channel.title: Flora May dela Cruz — Writing
channel.description: Notes from Flora May dela Cruz on product design, AI, accessibility, and refusing to ship things.

item.title: AI Made Me Question Where Roles Begin and End
item.description: Roles are not disappearing — they are getting more fluid. A note on what specialization actually protected, and why expertise becomes more visible, not less, when the tools get cheaper.

item.title: Building Complex Products Made Me Think Differently About Solutions and User Journeys
item.description: How organizations naturally organize around solutions and ownership, why customers never experience products that way, and what that gap keeps asking of the journey.

item.title: Visibility Is Valuable Until Customers Need to Move
item.description: A note on how products evolve from surfacing information to driving action — and why every step in that progression is gated by trust, not capability.

item.title: AI Changed How I Think About Design Artifacts
item.description: As AI moves from output generator to collaborator, the quality of our artifacts depends less on visual polish and more on structural clarity.

item.title: Three Labels, Six Surfaces
item.description: The smallest interaction rule I have, and the one I find myself repeating in every bulk-action review.

item.title: The Feature I Keep Refusing to Ship
item.description: A note on subtraction — why I write up the features a product pointedly does not have, and why the next reorg is the reason.

item.title: Notes from the Reversible Queue
item.description: Why I keep writing the same state machine into every enterprise product, and why Undo is the feature I argue hardest to keep.

item.title: Hello, Toybox
item.description: Why I'm building a Toybox in public — and what I hope it becomes.
